key_findings: LLM uses 7.5x more unique tokens than rule-based baseline (355 vs 47)
LLM descriptions average 9.0 tokens vs 6.1 for baseline
LLM naturally focuses on colors (63%) and ranks (62%), NOT patterns (8%)
Structural patterns like 'sorted', 'palindrome' rarely mentioned by blind LLM
This suggests naive observation doesn't naturally produce rule-useful descriptions
Discrimination ratio higher for LLM (2.20) but vocabulary much larger

implications.cognitive_plausibility: LLM describes surface features humans would notice first
implications.for_laps_training: May need to bias/guide descriptions toward rule-relevant features
implications.vocabulary_tradeoff: Smaller vocabulary (baseline) may be better for learning generalizable concepts
next_steps: Experiment with constrained LLM prompting to focus on patterns
Compare learning outcomes using LLM vs baseline descriptions
Consider hybrid approach: LLM generation with vocabulary constraints
